input.student_profile_summary: Grade 7, Science; visual learner; grade_level comprehension; strong in analytical tasks

short_narrative.student: You showed strong reasoning and communicated your ideas clearly. You're good at working through problems step by step.
short_narrative.parent: Your child demonstrated solid analytical thinking and communicated ideas well during this activity.
short_narrative.teacher: Student exhibits consistent grade-level reasoning with strong problem-understanding and communication across dimensions.

strongest_signals.student: Your best strengths are reasoning through problems and understanding what's being asked.
strongest_signals.parent: Your child's strongest areas are analytical reasoning and clear communication.
strongest_signals.teacher: Top signals: reasoning_strategy (3), problem_understanding (3), communication (3) — all at grade-level proficiency.

growth_areas.student: One area to grow: checking in on your own thinking as you work — what worked, what didn't.
growth_areas.parent: An area to develop: reflection and metacognition — pausing to think about how they learned.
growth_areas.teacher: Primary growth target: reflection_metacognition (2 — below other dimensions). Consider scaffolded self-assessment prompts.

expected_output.teacher_prompt: Scaffold metacognitive check-ins (e.g. 'what strategy did you use and why?') at key SPARK transitions to build reflection habit.
expected_output.teacher_takeaway: Strong analytical baseline; single growth lever is reflection_metacognition — targeted prompting should close the gap quickly.

metadata.notes: INTERIM GOLDEN FIXTURE — replace with Barb-reviewed tuple before arming the real gate (§11.6). Shape conforms to C19 (3 audience objects + teacher_prompt + teacher_takeaway).
